article_meta.title: By Ellie CookSenior Defense ReporterShareNewsweek is a Trust Project memberU.S

reasoning: The model identifies several genuine bias instances in the author's own prose and correctly avoids flagging quoted text. However, it uses bias type labels that are not from the defined list (Loaded Language, Hyperbolic Framing, Editorializing, Speculation instead of the valid types like Word Choice, Sensationalism/Emotionalism, Opinion Statements Presented as Fact, etc.). The identified text passages are largely defensible as author-level bias, but the type labels are wrong. There are also some debatable flagged items.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged items come from the author's own prose. No quoted text or attributed statements were flagged. Trump's quotes were correctly excluded.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most flagged items are defensible: 'rambling speech' is genuinely loaded author language, 'deepening the yawning chasm' is genuinely dramatic/sensational author framing, and 'unfathomable, until now' is genuinely speculative/editorial. The 'violation of international law' item is more debatable - it could be seen as a factual legal assessment rather than editorializing, though the lack of attribution is a fair point.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model caught the main bias instances. It could have also flagged 'mostly ice-covered expanse' as a potentially dismissive framing, but the major instances were identified. Could have noted the structural slant in how the article presents the issue predominantly from one angle.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All four bias type labels used (Loaded Language, Hyperbolic Framing, Editorializing, Speculation) are NOT from the defined list of valid bias types. 'Rambling speech' should be Word Choice or Subjective Qualifying Adjectives. 'Yawning chasm' should be Sensationalism/Emotionalism or Word Choice. 'Violation of international law' should be Opinion Statements Presented as Fact. 'Unfathomable, until now' should be Sensationalism/Emotionalism or Spin. This is a systematic labeling error.
